hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Convert JPEG, WebP, AVIF, and other images to PNG format. Lossless quality with transparency support—everything happens in your browser.

dropzone.supports: Supports JPEG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, BMP • Up to 10 images

formatSettings.title: Target Format Settings
formatSettings.selectionInfoEachImage: Each image will be converted to:
formatSettings.description: Select one or more target formats. When multiple formats are selected, each image will be converted to all selected formats and downloaded as a ZIP file. PNG preserves transparency losslessly, WebP offers excellent compression, AVIF provides the best compression, and JPEG has universal support.

qualitySettings.title: Quality Settings
qualitySettings.note: Applies to WebP, AVIF, and JPEG. Higher quality = larger files. 80% recommended. PNG is always lossless.

advancedSettings.title: Advanced Settings
advancedSettings.stripMetadataLabel: Strip metadata (EXIF, ICC) for smaller files
advancedSettings.stripMetadataDesc: Removing metadata can reduce file size but removes camera information, color profiles, and location data.

privacy.title: Data & Privacy
privacy.p1: Your images are processed entirely in your browser. No files are uploaded to any server.
privacy.p2: All PNG conversion happens locally using JavaScript libraries. Your images never leave your device.
privacy.p3: No analytics on image content; only anonymous aggregate performance counters may be collected for optimization.
privacy.p4: Close the tab any time after processing completes. Your privacy is guaranteed.
privacy.notice: All processing local + server-side transient, no files stored.

errors.selectImages: Please select image files (PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF, etc.)
errors.dropImages: Please drop image files (PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF, etc.)

howTo.title: How To Convert Images to PNG
howTo.step1Desc: Drop any JPEG, WebP, or AVIF graphic into the upload zone. The client canvas deconstructs the pixel grid locally, preparing the lossless extraction engine.
howTo.step2Desc: Hit convert and the in-browser PNG encoder maps every single pixel without discarding any visual data. Alpha transparency channels are generated automatically from supported inputs.
howTo.step3Desc: Download your newly minted PNGs one at a time or bundle the entire batch into a single ZIP archive. Zero quality degradation guaranteed.
howTo.tipText: PNG is ideal for graphics, logos, screenshots, and images requiring transparency. For photos, consider JPEG or WebP for smaller file sizes.

items.title: Zero-Loss Encoding
items.desc: Every single pixel survives the conversion intact. No compression artifacts, no color banding, no data thrown away.

items.title: Alpha Channel Native
items.desc: PNG carries full 8-bit transparency per pixel. Ideal for logos, icons, and layered composites.

items.title: Isolated Pipeline
items.desc: Encoding runs entirely inside a sandboxed browser canvas. No network calls, no cloud servers involved.

items.title: Instant Rasterization
items.desc: WebAssembly-powered codec fires within milliseconds. You see results before the progress bar finishes.

items.title: Batch Archiving
items.desc: Convert dozens of assets at once and pack them into a single compressed ZIP download.

items.title: Universal Rendering
items.desc: PNG is universally decoded by every browser, operating system, and print workflow in existence.

qualityGuide.title: Quality vs Size Guide
qualityGuide.interactiveIntro: Interactive demo: See how quality affects file size in real-time

items.question: What actually happens when I convert a JPEG to PNG?
items.answer: The browser decodes your JPEG's compressed pixel data onto an invisible HTML canvas, then re-encodes those raw pixels using the PNG deflate algorithm. This means every color value from the decoded JPEG is preserved exactly in the new PNG—you'll never lose additional quality during this step.

items.question: Will converting to PNG make my files larger?
items.answer: Often, yes. PNG uses lossless compression, which means it keeps every pixel intact instead of discarding visual data the way JPEG does. A 500KB JPEG photograph might become a 2-3MB PNG. That trade-off buys you perfect reproduction and editing flexibility.

items.question: Does this tool handle transparency when converting?
items.answer: If your source image (like a WebP or AVIF) contains an alpha transparency channel, the PNG output will preserve it completely. Sources without transparency (like standard JPEGs) will produce a fully opaque PNG since there's no alpha data to carry over.

items.question: Can I convert dozens of images at once?
items.answer: Yes. Drop your entire batch into the upload zone and the WebAssembly encoder will process them sequentially at full speed. Once finished, grab everything in a single ZIP download rather than clicking through files individually.

items.question: Are my images uploaded to any server during conversion?
items.answer: Not at all. The entire PNG encoding pipeline runs inside your browser's sandboxed JavaScript runtime. Your source files never leave your device, and no network requests carry image data. Close the tab and every trace disappears.
